fields.summary: Add ability to use interactive shell with normal yarn container
[Umbrella] Support Interactive Docker Shell to running Containers

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: This task is focusing on extending interactive shell capability to yarn container without docker.  This will improve some aspect of debugging mapreduce or spark applications.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: First patch renames run_docker_with_pty to exec_container to support both docker exec -it and yarn container.  Yarn container is using restricted shell to prevent user from going out of bound.  DefaultLinuxContainerRuntime implements writing .cmd file for c version of container-executor to run shell for yarn container.

+1 for patch 3. Thanks for working on this, [~eyang]! See also YARN-9117 which will provide a helpful follow-on patch. I also noticed some cleanup we could do in container-executor.c (freeing allocated memory, valgrind leaks in the test suite, and error logging, mainly not introduced by this patch). So let's do that in another ticket as well.

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #15601 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/15601/])
YARN-8962. Add ability to use interactive shell with normal yarn (billie: rev 72e7c6a489858ea48e94dc015752de620f8d6da1)

comments.body: First patch renames run_docker_with_pty to exec_container to support both docker exec -it and yarn container.  Yarn container is using restricted shell to prevent user from going out of bound.  DefaultLinuxContainerRuntime implements writing .cmd file for c version of container-executor to run shell for yarn container.
